title: mentha longifolia var. lavandulacea
intro: Mentha longifolia var. lavandulacea, also known as Hoary Mint, is a subspecies of the peppermint plant, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves with a distinctive lavender tint. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hoary Mint include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to reduce stress and anxiety. Hoary Mint can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies and aromatherapy applications.
